summary: Core feature (dict/list as native Data-holding types, signed elements survive the application/plang wire, set rebinds) is correctly implemented and fully green. NEEDS WORK on the Stage 4-5 'one compare path' goal: two live divergences inside the new Compare.cs/Operator.cs that the green suites do not catch.

findings.title: text equality is case-insensitive but text ordering is case-sensitive
findings.detail: 'a' == 'A' is true yet 'a' > 'A' is also true — trichotomy violated; sort and unique disagree. Impl also contradicts its own doc ('text lexical/invariant'). Fix: Order text arm should use string.Compare(a,b,OrdinalIgnoreCase).

findings.title: contains/in use a second divergent equality path
findings.detail: ==/group/unique compare dict/list structurally via Compare.AreEqual; contains/in use the local Operator.AreEqual (reference equality for dict/list). %list% contains %dict% returns false for a structurally-equal dict while == returns true. Route ContainsElement through Compare.AreEqual; delete the local helper.

findings.title: dead code: Operator.Compare(object?,object?)
findings.detail: Zero callers after ordering relocated to Compare.Order (assert's Compare is its own local helper). Remove.

findings.title: legacy List<object?> branches in sort/unique bypass the one-compare-path
findings.detail: sort legacy uses Comparer<object>.Default; unique legacy uses Distinct(). Divergent from Compare.Order/AreEqual (nulls-last, mixed-type-throw, structural equality lost). Either dead (delete) or reachable+divergent (unify).

findings.title: Wire.IsDataShaped name+value heuristic is fragile
findings.detail: Lifts any array element with name+value keys to a Data envelope. Safe for the wire it serves but keys off user-pluggable field names; prefer keying on type/signature presence.

notes: Compare unification is partial by plan scope (row G named only condition operators + data.sort): assert.greaterThan/lessThan still use a private Compare (assert/code/Default.cs:185), pre-existing and outside this diff. Flagged for tracking, not counted as a regression.
